original_abstract: In this letter, we introduce a practical non-linear energy harvesting model and develop a specialized resource allocation algorithm for simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. The design is framed as a non-convex optimization problem aimed at maximizing the total power harvested by energy receivers, while ensuring that multiple information receivers meet their minimum signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) requirements. To solve this, we transform the non-convex objective function from a complex sum-of-ratios form into an equivalent subtractive form, facilitating the derivation of an efficient iterative algorithm. Throughout the iterations, we address rank-constrained semidefinite programs (SDPs) by leveraging SDP relaxation to reach optimal solutions. Our numerical results demonstrate that accounting for non-linear energy harvesting characteristics yields substantial performance gains over designs based on traditional linear models.
humanized_abstract: In this letter, we introduce a practical non-linear energy harvesting model and proposed an efficient resource allocation algorithm for simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. Based on the non-linear energy harvesting model, the considered SWIPT system is formulated into a non-convex optimization problem to maximize the total energy harvested at the energy receivers (ERs) while satisfying the required signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) for the information receivers (IRs). To obtain the optimal solution, the non-convex objective function with sum-of-ratios structure is first reformulated into an equivalent subtractive form by using some mathematical manipulations and algebraic transformations, which leads to deriving an efficient iterative algorithm. The rank constraint in the SDPs involved in the iterations is addressed by utilizing the SDP relaxation method. Simulation results show that the incorporation of non-linear energy harvesting (EH) model significantly outperforms that of linear EH model.

The rank constraint in the SDPs involved in the iterations is addressed by utilizing the SDP relaxation method. Simulation results show that the incorporation of non-linear energy harvesting (EH) model significantly outperforms that of linear EH model.
